steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: Left of the blank sits the singular noun phrase Food writer J. Kenji López‑Alt (with the relative clause who explores the science behind cooking). Right of the blank begins the clause that lower boiling points at higher elevations can wreak all sorts of havoc on recipes.

steps.title: Apply subject‑verb agreement
steps.content: Because the subject is singular, the verb must be singular and in the present tense: explains. The other three options either use a plural verb (are explaining, explain, have explained) or an incorrect tense. That matches choice B — B is correct.
